vulnerability.title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log Directory
vulnerability.description: To properly set the owner of /var/log, run the command: $ sudo chown root /var/log
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: newown="" if id "0" >/dev/null 2>&1; then newown="0" fi if [[ -z "$newown" ]]; then >&2 echo "0 is not a defined user on the system" else find -P /var/log/ -maxdepth 0 -type d ! -user 0 -exec chown --no-dereference "$newown" {} \; fi

triage_decision.reason: Changing the owner of /var/log to root is a standard STIG requirement and does not affect authentication, networking, or critical boot components. The root account always exists on RHEL systems, so the chown operation is unlikely to cause lockout or service disruption.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Potential for incorrect user ID if root is somehow missing (unlikely); Impact limited to file ownership metadata
